title: baccharis poeppigiana subsp. ocellata
intro: Baccharis poeppigiana subsp. ocellata is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, commonly known as a shrub or small tree that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Baccharis poeppigiana subsp. ocellata include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
